inputs.label: Number of Leaks
inputs.businessContext: Total number of compressed air leaks identified. Based on ISO 11011 standard for compressed air energy audits.

inputs.label: Average Leak Size
inputs.businessContext: Equivalent orifice diameter of typical leaks. Common sizes: 1mm (small), 3mm (medium), 5mm (large), 10mm (very large). Based on compressed air leak quantification methods.

inputs.label: Operating Hours per Day
inputs.businessContext: Hours per day the compressed air system operates. Used to calculate annual energy consumption.

inputs.label: Operating Days per Year
inputs.businessContext: Number of days per year the system runs. Typical industrial operation: 250 days (5 days/week, 50 weeks).

inputs.label: Electricity Cost per kWh
inputs.businessContext: Average electricity rate. Varies by region; typical US industrial rate ~$0.12/kWh. Source: EIA.

inputs.label: Compressor Specific Power
inputs.businessContext: Power required to produce 1 cfm of compressed air. Typical range 0.18-0.35 kW/cfm depending on compressor type and efficiency. Based on DOE compressed air guidelines.

inputs.label: Leak Flow Rate per Size
inputs.businessContext: Flow rate for a given leak size at standard pressure (100 psi). Pre-calculated based on orifice size: 1mm=1.5 cfm, 3mm=11 cfm, 5mm=30 cfm, 10mm=120 cfm. Source: Compressed Air Challenge.

inputs.label: Data Confidence Level
inputs.businessContext: Confidence in input data accuracy. Affects adjustment factor: low=0.8, medium=1.0, high=1.2. Used for risk-adjusted cost estimation.

thresholds.annualCost: annualCost > 10000 ? 'High leak cost: immediate action recommended' : ''

hiddenLossDrivers: annualCost > 10000 ? 'High leak cost: immediate action recommended' : ''
suggestedActions: Conduct ultrasonic leak detection to pinpoint leaks
Consider installing variable speed drives on compressors

premiumFeatures: Detailed breakdown by leak size category
